items.title: A 5,000 Year Old Cold Case With a Terrifying Twist
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>Scientists have long used fossils and ancient human remains to uncover what life was like in the distant past, but some discoveries come with chilling consequences. When Ötzi the Iceman, Europe’s oldest known natural human mummy, was found frozen in the Alps, it shocked the world. But even more disturbing? A series of mysterious and bizarre deaths linked to those who studied him. Is it coincidence… or a real-life mummy curse?

Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#ötzi #paranormal #weirdhistory</article>

items.title: Eric Swalwell's "best friend" regrets defending him
items.author: Vox
items.content:
<article>Sen. Ruben Gallego (D-AZ) regrets defending Eric Swalwell, who resigned from Congress following sexual assault allegations.

 ”This is the kind of thing that makes all of us relook at what we have been accepting, versus not accepting. ” Gallego told Vox’s Astead Herndon.

Listen to the full interview with Gallego on America, Actually, on Saturday, April 25 here on YouTube or wherever you get your podcasts.

Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.

Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE
Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom
Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/
Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts</article>
